sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, the question is whether the payoff is worth the plate space.
sections.subtitleText: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, the question is whether the payoff is worth the plate space.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 3g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
sections.subtitleText: 3g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 0.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: 0.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 17% daily value, vitamin a at 4% daily value, vitamin c at 3% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin k at 17% daily value, vitamin a at 4% daily value, vitamin c at 3% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: adds hydrating volume because it can bulk out meals with almost no calories. easy salad base because very practical in sandwiches, burgers, and salads. mild and easy to tolerate because a practical plus for people who dislike bitter greens.

sections.subtitleText: the upsides first: adds hydrating volume because it can bulk out meals with almost no calories. easy salad base because very practical in sandwiches, burgers, and salads. mild and easy to tolerate because a practical plus for people who dislike bitter greens.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: micronutrient payoff is weak because that is a major problem in a category meant to reward nutrient density. fibre payoff is weak for a vegetable because it does not offer much of the maintenance-and-repair value the category is supposed to reward. mostly water with limited nutritional return because very low payoff compared with stronger vegetables.

closing.summary: Iceberg Lettuce does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 12 from a base nutrition score of 21. Context dragged it down by 9 points.
